subject: [PATCH 01/14] mm: decouple protnone helpers from CONFIG_NUMA_BALANCING

2026-04-27.patch_summary: This patch decouples the protnone helpers from CONFIG_NUMA_BALANCING by introducing a new configuration option, CONFIG_ARCH_HAS_PTE_PROTNONE. This allows architectures that support present-but-inaccessible page table entries (protnone PTEs) to select this option independently of NUMA balancing. The six supported architectures are updated to select this option, and the protnone infrastructure is made available for use beyond NUMA balancing, such as in userfaultfd working set tracking. No functional changes are introduced, but the infrastructure is now decoupled from NUMA balancing.

reviews.author: SeongJae Park
reviews.summary:
Reviewer agrees with the patch's usefulness beyond NUMA balancing, and is actively using it for their own project (DAMON) but didn't provide any specific technical feedback or suggestions.

Reviewer SeongJae Park expressed support for the patch, considering it a good change that makes the infrastructure available for userfaultfd and general access monitoring purposes.

Reviewer noted that the patch does not update the comment in include/linux/pgtable.h to reflect the new CONFIG_ARCH_HAS_PTE_PROTNONE option, and requested the update
reviews.raw_body:
I agree.  It can be useful for general access monitoring.  I'm also slowly but
continuously working [1] to use that for DAMON.

---

I think this is a good change for making the infrastructure available for uffd.
I believe this can also be useful for making it further available for general
access monitoring purposes.  I hope this to be landed on the mainline sooner
than later.  Thank you for doing this, Kiryl!

---

Acked-by: SeongJae Park <sj@kernel.org>

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/linux-mm/20260218054320.4570-1-sj@kernel.org/

Thanks,
SJ

[...]

2026-04-29.patch_summary: This patch decouples the protnone helpers from CONFIG_NUMA_BALANCING by introducing a new configuration option, CONFIG_ARCH_HAS_PTE_PROTNONE. This allows architectures that support present-but-inaccessible page table entries (protnone PTEs) to select this option independently of NUMA balancing. The six supported architectures are updated to select this option, and the infrastructure is made available for use beyond NUMA balancing, such as in userfaultfd working set tracking. No functional changes are introduced, but the protnone support is now decoupled from NUMA balancing.
